title: Maguindanao massacre
description:
58 people, including 32 journalists, were killed in Ampatuan, Maguindanao. The massacre was perpetrated by members of the Ampatuan political clan to prevent a rival from filing a candidacy. In 2019, the principal accused were found guilty.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maguindanao_massacre)

title: Vizconde massacre
description:
Estrellita Vizconde and her two daughters were found murdered in their home in Paranaque, Metro Manila. The case went through multiple trials and appeals, becoming one of the most controversial criminal cases in Philippine history.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vizconde_massacre)

title: Chiong sisters disappearance
description:
Sisters Marijoy and Jacqueline Chiong were abducted in Cebu City. Marijoy's body was later found in a ravine. Seven men, including Francisco Juan Larranaga, were convicted. The case drew international attention regarding the death penalty.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chiong_case)

title: Murder of Munir Said Thalib
description:
Indonesian human rights activist Munir Said Thalib was poisoned with arsenic on a Garuda Indonesia flight from Jakarta to Amsterdam. Pollycarpus Priyanto, an off-duty pilot, was convicted. The case exposed links to Indonesian intelligence.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Munir_Said_Thalib)

title: 2002 Bali bombings
description:
A series of terrorist bombings struck the tourist district of Kuta, Bali, killing 202 people from over 20 countries. The attacks were carried out by members of Jemaah Islamiyah. Three perpetrators were executed in 2008.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2002_Bali_bombings)

title: Murder of Wayan Mirna Salihin
description:
Jessica Kumala Wongso was convicted of murdering her friend Wayan Mirna Salihin by lacing her Vietnamese iced coffee with cyanide at a Jakarta cafe. The highly publicized trial resulted in a 20-year prison sentence.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murder_of_Wayan_Mirna_Salihin)

title: Murder of Altantuya Shaariibuu
description:
Mongolian model and translator Altantuya Shaariibuu was murdered and her body destroyed with C-4 explosives near Kuala Lumpur. Two police commandos were convicted. The case became a major political scandal in Malaysia.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murder_of_Altantuya_Shaariibuu)

title: Disappearance of Nora Quoirin
description:
15-year-old Franco-Irish teenager Nora Quoirin disappeared from a resort in Negeri Sembilan, Malaysia. Her body was found 10 days later near a waterfall. The Malaysian coroner returned an open verdict, and the circumstances remain disputed.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Death_of_Nora_Quoirin)

title: Murder of Kevin Morais
description:
Deputy public prosecutor Kevin Morais was abducted and murdered in Kuala Lumpur. His body was found in a concrete-filled oil drum. An army pathologist and six accomplices were convicted. The case raised concerns about threats to legal officials.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murder_of_Kevin_Morais)

title: Toa Payoh ritual murders
description:
Two children were kidnapped and murdered in ritual killings in the Toa Payoh neighborhood of Singapore. Medium Adrian Lim and his two accomplices were convicted and executed in 1988. The case is one of Singapore's most notorious crimes.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Adrian_Lim)

title: Curry murder case
description:
Ayamah Annamalai murdered her husband's mistress and cooked the body parts in a curry to dispose of the evidence. The case in Singapore became infamous and was discovered when remains were found. She was convicted of murder.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ayamah_case)

title: Kallang body parts murder
description:
Dismembered body parts of a Nigerian national were found in the Kallang River, Singapore. Took Leng How was convicted of murdering the victim and sentenced to death. The case drew attention for its gruesome nature.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Took_Leng_How)

title: Bac Viet Bank robbery
description:
A man armed with a fake bomb and a firearm robbed a branch of Techcombank in Ho Chi Minh City. The robber was identified and arrested within hours using security camera footage. The case highlighted banking security concerns in Vietnam.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crime_in_Vietnam)

title: Nam Cam case
description:
Truong Van Cam, known as Nam Cam, was a Vietnamese crime boss who ran a vast criminal network in Ho Chi Minh City. He was convicted of ordering the murder of a rival and corruption. He and several accomplices were executed in 2004.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Truong_Van_Cam)

title: Le Van Luyen murders
description:
Le Van Luyen murdered a gold shop owner, her daughter, and her infant grandchild during a robbery in Bac Giang Province. He was arrested after a manhunt and sentenced to death. The case shocked Vietnam due to the brutality of the crimes.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crime_in_Vietnam)

title: Murder of Qandeel Baloch
description:
Pakistani social media celebrity Qandeel Baloch was strangled to death by her brother Waseem in Multan, Punjab, in a so-called honor killing. Her brother was convicted and sentenced to life imprisonment in 2019.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Qandeel_Baloch)

title: Javed Iqbal serial murders
description:
Javed Iqbal confessed to the murder of 100 boys in Lahore, Pakistan, between 1998 and 1999. He dissolved the victims' bodies in acid. He was sentenced to death but was found dead in his cell before execution.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Javed_Iqbal_(serial_killer))

title: Murder of Daniel Pearl
description:
American journalist Daniel Pearl of The Wall Street Journal was kidnapped and murdered in Karachi, Pakistan. Ahmed Omar Saeed Sheikh and others were convicted. The case became a symbol of the dangers faced by journalists.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Daniel_Pearl)

title: Rana Plaza collapse
description:
The Rana Plaza garment factory building collapsed in Dhaka, Bangladesh, killing 1,134 workers. Building owner Sohel Rana and others were charged with murder due to negligence. The disaster led to global reforms in garment industry safety.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2013_Rana_Plaza_factory_collapse)

title: Murder of Fahad and Raihan
description:
Rifat Sharif was hacked to death in broad daylight in Barguna, Bangladesh, in front of his wife. The attack was captured on video and went viral. The main perpetrator and his accomplices were arrested and convicted.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murder_of_Rifat_Sharif)

title: Murder of Avijit Roy
description:
Bangladeshi-American blogger and author Avijit Roy was hacked to death at the Dhaka book fair. His wife was also severely injured. Members of the extremist group Ansarullah Bangla Team were convicted of the murder.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avijit_Roy)

title: Central Bank of Sri Lanka bombing
description:
A truck bomb detonated by the LTTE near the Central Bank of Sri Lanka in Colombo killed 91 people and injured over 1,400. It was one of the deadliest terrorist attacks in Sri Lankan history.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_Bank_bombing)

title: Murder of Wasim Thajudeen
description:
Sri Lankan rugby player Wasim Thajudeen died in a suspicious car accident in Colombo, initially ruled accidental. Later forensic evidence revealed blunt force injuries, and the case was reclassified as murder. It remains under investigation.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wasim_Thajudeen)

title: 2019 Sri Lanka Easter bombings
description:
A series of coordinated suicide bombings targeted three churches and three luxury hotels in Colombo and other cities, killing 269 people. The attacks were carried out by a local Islamist group. Numerous suspects were arrested.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2019_Sri_Lanka_Easter_bombings)

title: Sobhraj murders in Nepal
description:
Serial killer Charles Sobhraj murdered multiple Western tourists along the Asian hippie trail, including victims in Kathmandu, Nepal. He was convicted in Nepal in 2004 for the murder of American backpacker Connie Jo Bronzich.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Charles_Sobhraj)

title: Murder of Nirmala Panta
description:
13-year-old Nirmala Panta was raped and murdered in Kanchanpur, Nepal. The case sparked nationwide protests demanding justice. Despite arrests, the investigation was widely criticized for mishandling evidence and remains controversial.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murder_of_Nirmala_Panta)

title: Royal massacre of Nepal
description:
Crown Prince Dipendra of Nepal shot and killed King Birendra and eight other members of the royal family at the Narayanhiti Palace in Kathmandu before fatally shooting himself. The massacre led to a political crisis.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nepalese_royal_massacre)

title: Serial murders of Saeed Hanaei
description:
Saeed Hanaei, known as the Spider Killer, murdered at least 16 women in Mashhad, Iran, between 2000 and 2001, claiming a religious duty to cleanse the city. He was arrested, tried, and executed in 2002.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saeed_Hanaei)

title: Chain murders of Iran
description:
A series of murders of Iranian dissident intellectuals and writers in the late 1990s, including Dariush Forouhar and his wife. The killings were traced to rogue agents within Iran's Ministry of Intelligence.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chain_murders_of_Iran)

title: Murder of Neda Agha-Soltan
description:
26-year-old Neda Agha-Soltan was shot and killed during the Iranian election protests in Tehran. Her death was captured on video and became a symbol of the Iranian Green Movement. No one has been held accountable.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Death_of_Neda_Agha-Soltan)

title: Camp Speicher massacre
description:
ISIL militants captured and executed between 1,095 and 1,700 unarmed Iraqi Air Force cadets at Camp Speicher near Tikrit, Iraq. Mass graves were later uncovered. Multiple perpetrators were tried and sentenced to death by Iraqi courts.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Camp_Speicher_massacre)

title: Assassination of Hisham al-Hashimi
description:
Iraqi security analyst and government adviser Hisham al-Hashimi was shot dead outside his home in Baghdad by gunmen on motorcycles. Members of an Iran-backed militia were arrested in connection with the assassination.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hisham_al-Hashimi)

title: 2016 Karrada bombing
description:
A truck bomb detonated in the busy Karrada shopping district of Baghdad, killing 341 people. ISIL claimed responsibility for the attack, which was the deadliest single bombing in Iraq. Security officials were held accountable.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2016_Karrada_bombing)

title: Murder of Jamal Khashoggi
description:
Saudi journalist Jamal Khashoggi was murdered inside the Saudi consulate in Istanbul, Turkey, by a team of Saudi agents. The killing provoked an international outcry. Several Saudi agents were convicted in a Saudi court.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Jamal_Khashoggi)

title: 2009 Jeddah floods criminal negligence
description:
Catastrophic flooding in Jeddah killed over 120 people, caused largely by negligent urban planning and blocked drainage systems. Saudi authorities arrested several officials and contractors on charges of corruption and criminal negligence.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2009_Jeddah_floods)

title: Al-Khobar massacre
description:
A truck bomb was detonated outside the Khobar Towers housing complex used by US Air Force personnel in Saudi Arabia, killing 19 Americans and injuring 498. Members of Saudi Hezbollah were indicted for the attack.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Khobar_Towers_bombing)

title: Murder of three sisters in Fujairah
description:
Three Emirati sisters were murdered in their home in Fujairah by their brother in an honor killing case that drew significant attention to domestic violence in the UAE. The perpetrator was convicted and sentenced.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crime_in_the_United_Arab_Emirates)

title: Assassination of Mahmoud Al-Mabhouh
description:
Hamas commander Mahmoud Al-Mabhouh was found dead in his Dubai hotel room. CCTV footage revealed a sophisticated assassination by a team using forged passports. The killing was widely attributed to Mossad but no one was formally charged.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Mahmoud_Al-Mabhouh)

title: Murder of Ibolya Ryan
description:
Romanian-American teacher Ibolya Ryan was stabbed to death in a restroom at the Boutik Mall in Abu Dhabi by a fully veiled attacker. The perpetrator, Dalal al-Hashemi, was convicted and executed in 2015.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murder_of_Ibolya_Ryan)

title: Assassination of Yitzhak Rabin
description:
Israeli Prime Minister Yitzhak Rabin was assassinated at a peace rally in Tel Aviv by right-wing extremist Yigal Amir. The assassination profoundly impacted the Israeli-Palestinian peace process. Amir was convicted and sentenced to life.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Yitzhak_Rabin)

title: Murder of Tair Rada
description:
13-year-old Tair Rada was found murdered in a locked bathroom stall at her school in Katzrin, Israel. Roman Zadorov was convicted but maintained his innocence; the case became one of Israel's most debated criminal convictions.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murder_of_Tair_Rada)

title: Tel Aviv Dolphinarium massacre
description:
A suicide bomber detonated explosives outside the Dolphinarium nightclub in Tel Aviv, killing 21 people, mostly teenagers. Hamas claimed responsibility for the attack. The bombing was one of the deadliest attacks during the Second Intifada.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dolphinarium_discotheque_massacre)

title: Assassination of Nahed Hattar
description:
Jordanian writer Nahed Hattar was shot dead outside the Palace of Justice in Amman by a gunman angered by a cartoon Hattar had shared on social media. The shooter was convicted and sentenced to death.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nahed_Hattar)

title: 2005 Amman hotel bombings
description:
Three coordinated suicide bombings struck hotels in Amman, Jordan, killing 60 people and injuring 115. Al-Qaeda in Iraq, led by Abu Musab al-Zarqawi, claimed responsibility. Several accomplices were arrested and convicted.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2005_Amman_bombings)

title: Karak Castle attack
description:
Gunmen attacked the historic Karak Castle and surrounding area in Jordan, killing 10 people including a Canadian tourist and several Jordanian security personnel. Four attackers were killed in the ensuing siege. ISIL claimed responsibility.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2016_Jordanian_tourist_attack)

title: Assassination of Rafic Hariri
description:
Former Lebanese Prime Minister Rafic Hariri was killed along with 21 others by a massive car bomb in Beirut. The UN Special Tribunal for Lebanon convicted Hezbollah member Salim Ayyash in absentia in 2020.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Rafic_Hariri)

title: 2020 Beirut explosion
description:
A massive explosion at the Port of Beirut killed at least 218 people and devastated large parts of the city. The blast was caused by improperly stored ammonium nitrate. The investigation has been repeatedly obstructed and no one has been held accountable.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2020_Beirut_explosion)

title: Assassination of Gebran Tueni
description:
Lebanese journalist and politician Gebran Tueni was killed by a car bomb in Mkalles, near Beirut. He had been an outspoken critic of Syrian interference in Lebanon. The assassination is linked to a series of political killings.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gebran_Tueni)

title: Chernobyl nuclear disaster criminal negligence
description:
The Chernobyl nuclear disaster in the Ukrainian SSR killed dozens directly and affected millions. Plant director Viktor Bryukhanov and others were convicted of criminal negligence for safety violations that caused the explosion.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chernobyl_disaster)

title: Murder of Georgiy Gongadze
description:
Ukrainian journalist Georgiy Gongadze was abducted and murdered. His headless body was found in a forest outside Kyiv. Police general Oleksiy Pukach was convicted. Audio recordings implicated high-ranking officials in ordering the killing.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Georgiy_Gongadze)

title: Assassination of Pavel Sheremet
description:
Belarusian-born journalist Pavel Sheremet was killed by a car bomb in central Kyiv. Despite suspects being charged, the case has not resulted in a definitive conviction and remains highly controversial.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Pavel_Sheremet)

title: Disappearance of Elodia Ghinescu
description:
Romanian lawyer Elodia Ghinescu disappeared from her home in Brasov. Her husband Cristian Cioaca, a police officer, was convicted of her murder despite the body never being found, based on forensic evidence found in their home.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Disappearance_of_Elodia_Ghinescu)

title: Colectiv nightclub fire
description:
A fire at the Colectiv nightclub in Bucharest killed 65 people and injured 146. The fire started from pyrotechnics during a concert. The club owners and Bucharest mayor were convicted of criminal negligence.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Colectiv_nightclub_fire)

title: Caracal kidnapping case
description:
Two teenage girls, Alexandra Macesanu and Luiza Melencu, were kidnapped and murdered by Gheorghe Dinca in Caracal, Romania. The case exposed severe failings in the Romanian emergency response system when police delayed responding to calls.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caracal_kidnapping_case)

title: Murder of Eva Biszku
description:
The trial of Bela Biszku, a former Hungarian interior minister, for his role in the brutal repression and murder of civilians after the 1956 Hungarian Revolution. He was convicted but died before sentencing was finalized.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/B%C3%A9la_Biszku)

title: Romani serial murders
description:
Between 2008 and 2009, a series of racially motivated attacks targeted Romani communities across Hungary, killing six people including a child. Four perpetrators were convicted. The case highlighted racial violence against Roma in Hungary.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2008%E2%80%932009_attacks_against_Roma_in_Hungary)

title: West Balkany robbery
description:
Hungary's largest bank heist took place when robbers broke into a Postabank branch in Budapest through underground tunnels, stealing hundreds of millions of forints. The elaborate heist was eventually solved and the perpetrators arrested.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crime_in_Hungary)

title: Murder of Caroline Crouch
description:
British woman Caroline Crouch was suffocated by her husband Babis Anagnostopoulos in their Athens home. He initially staged a fake burglary but was caught after data from fitness trackers contradicted his account. He was convicted.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murder_of_Caroline_Crouch)

title: Assassination of Alexandros Grigoropoulos
description:
15-year-old Alexandros Grigoropoulos was shot and killed by police officer Epaminondas Korkoneas in the Exarcheia district of Athens, sparking weeks of riots across Greece. Korkoneas was convicted of murder.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Killing_of_Alexandros_Grigoropoulos)

title: Murder of Pavlos Fyssas
description:
Anti-fascist rapper Pavlos Fyssas was stabbed to death in Piraeus, Athens, by a member of the neo-Nazi Golden Dawn party. The murder led to a major trial of Golden Dawn's leadership, who were convicted of running a criminal organization.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murder_of_Pavlos_Fyssas)

title: Disappearance of Madeleine McCann
description:
Three-year-old British girl Madeleine McCann disappeared from a holiday apartment in Praia da Luz, Algarve, Portugal. The case became one of the most widely reported missing persons cases in history. German suspect Christian Brueckner was later identified.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Disappearance_of_Madeleine_McCann)

title: Casa Pia child abuse scandal
description:
A widespread child sexual abuse scandal at the Casa Pia orphanage network in Lisbon, Portugal, was exposed. Several prominent figures including a TV presenter and diplomat were convicted. The abuse spanned decades.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Casa_Pia_child_sexual_abuse_scandal)

title: Murder of Maria Fernandes
description:
The murder of a Portuguese woman by her ex-boyfriend in Lisbon drew widespread attention to domestic violence in Portugal and prompted legislative reforms regarding restraining orders and victim protection.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Domestic_violence_in_Portugal)

title: Jokela school shooting
description:
18-year-old Pekka-Eric Auvinen opened fire at Jokela High School in Tuusula, Finland, killing eight people before turning the gun on himself. The shooting was the first school massacre in Finland and prompted gun law reforms.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jokela_school_shooting)

title: Kauhajoki school shooting
description:
22-year-old Matti Juhani Saari shot and killed 10 people at the Seinajoki University of Applied Sciences in Kauhajoki, Finland, before killing himself. The shooting came less than a year after Jokela and intensified debates over firearms.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kauhajoki_school_shooting)

title: Lake Bodom murders
description:
Four teenagers were attacked while camping at Lake Bodom near Espoo, Finland. Three were killed and one survived. Despite a 2004 trial of the sole survivor Nils Gustafsson, who was acquitted, the case remains one of Finland's most famous unsolved crimes.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lake_Bodom_murders)

title: Murder of Kim Wall
description:
Swedish journalist Kim Wall was murdered aboard a homemade submarine by Danish inventor Peter Madsen in Copenhagen harbor. Madsen dismembered her body and disposed of it at sea. He was convicted and sentenced to life in prison.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murder_of_Kim_Wall)

title: 2015 Copenhagen shootings
description:
Omar El-Hussein carried out two shooting attacks in Copenhagen, first at a free speech event and then at a synagogue, killing two people. He was shot dead by police in a subsequent confrontation.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2015_Copenhagen_shootings)

title: Blekingegade Gang robberies
description:
The Blekingegade Gang was a Marxist-Leninist group that carried out a series of violent robberies in Denmark in the 1980s, including a post office heist that resulted in the death of a police officer. Members were convicted in 1991.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blekingegade_Gang)

title: Murder of Elaine O'Hara
description:
Dublin childcare worker Elaine O'Hara disappeared and her remains were found over a year later in the Dublin Mountains. Architect Graham Dwyer was convicted of her murder. Text messages revealed a disturbing pattern of manipulation.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murder_of_Elaine_O%27Hara)

title: Murder of Veronica Guerin
description:
Investigative journalist Veronica Guerin was shot dead in her car at a traffic light in Dublin by a hitman working for drug lord John Gilligan. The murder led to the establishment of the Criminal Assets Bureau in Ireland.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Veronica_Guerin)

title: Murder of Sophie Toscan du Plantier
description:
French film producer Sophie Toscan du Plantier was found beaten to death outside her holiday home in West Cork, Ireland. British journalist Ian Bailey was convicted in absentia by a French court in 2019 but was never extradited.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murder_of_Sophie_Toscan_du_Plantier)

title: Christchurch mosque shootings
description:
Australian white supremacist Brenton Tarrant attacked two mosques in Christchurch, New Zealand, killing 51 people. He was sentenced to life without parole, the first such sentence in New Zealand history.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Christchurch_mosque_shootings)

title: Bain family murders
description:
Five members of the Bain family were shot dead in their Dunedin home. David Bain was convicted but later acquitted on retrial in 2009. Whether David or his father Robin committed the murders remains one of New Zealand's most debated cases.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bain_family_murders)

title: Crewe murders
description:
Harvey and Jeannette Crewe were murdered at their farm in Pukekawa, New Zealand. Arthur Allan Thomas was twice convicted and later pardoned after evidence of police planting was discovered. The actual killer was never identified.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crewe_murders)

title: Murder of Zoran Djindjic
description:
Serbian Prime Minister Zoran Djindjic was assassinated by a sniper outside the Serbian government building in Belgrade. The Zemun clan, a criminal organization with ties to secret police, was convicted. It was a watershed moment in Serbian politics.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Zoran_%C4%90in%C4%91i%C4%87)

title: Murder of Ivan Stambolic
description:
Former Serbian president Ivan Stambolic disappeared while jogging in Belgrade. His body was found in 2003 in a lime pit. Members of the Special Operations Unit were convicted, and the murder was linked to Slobodan Milosevic's regime.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ivan_Stamboli%C4%87)

title: Arkan's assassination
description:
Zeljko Raznatovic, known as Arkan, a Serbian paramilitary commander and war crimes suspect, was shot dead in the lobby of the Intercontinental Hotel in Belgrade. While several people were convicted, the mastermind was never identified.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C5%BDeljko_Raznatovi%C4%87)

title: Murder of Ivana and Kristina Grcevic
description:
Ivan Bjelanic murdered two teenage sisters in Zagreb, Croatia, before taking his own life. The domestic violence case prompted significant public discussion about protective measures for victims of domestic abuse in Croatia.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crime_in_Croatia)

title: Murder of Ivana Hodak
description:
Croatian law student Ivana Hodak was shot dead in front of her Zagreb apartment building. The murder was connected to organized crime and her father's legal work. The killer was identified as a contract assassin and convicted.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murder_of_Ivana_Hodak)

title: Zagreb post office robbery
description:
A major armed robbery of a post office in Zagreb was one of Croatia's most brazen heists. The perpetrators used military-grade weapons and were later identified through forensic evidence and security footage.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crime_in_Croatia)

title: Murder of Georgi Markov
description:
Bulgarian dissident writer Georgi Markov was assassinated in London by a poisoned pellet fired from an umbrella, attributed to the Bulgarian secret service with KGB assistance. The case remains officially unsolved.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Georgi_Markov)

title: 2012 Burgas bus bombing
description:
A suicide bomber attacked a bus carrying Israeli tourists at Burgas Airport, Bulgaria, killing five Israelis and the Bulgarian bus driver. Hezbollah was implicated and two suspects were tried in absentia and convicted.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2012_Burgas_bus_bombing)

title: Murder of Boris Tsankov
description:
Bulgarian journalist and radio host Boris Tsankov was shot dead in downtown Sofia. He had previously reported on organized crime. Despite initial arrests, the case has not resulted in a conviction and remains open.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boris_Tsankov)

title: Kidnapping of Michal Kovac Jr.
description:
Michal Kovac Jr., son of Slovak President Michal Kovac, was abducted from Bratislava and taken to Austria. The kidnapping was attributed to the Slovak Intelligence Service under Vladimir Meciar's government. An amnesty prevented prosecution.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kidnapping_of_Michal_Kov%C3%A1%C4%8D_Jr.)

title: Murder of Jan Kuciak
description:
Slovak investigative journalist Jan Kuciak and his fiancee Martina Kusnirova were shot dead in their home near Bratislava. The case led to massive protests and the resignation of the Prime Minister. Businessman Marian Kocner was acquitted but associates were convicted.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murder_of_J%C3%A1n_Kuciak)

title: Murder of Robert Remiasi
description:
Robert Remiasi, a key witness in the Kovac kidnapping case, was killed in a car bomb explosion in Bratislava. The murder was widely believed to be connected to the intelligence services. It remains officially unsolved.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Robert_Remia%C5%A1)

title: Hijacking of the 13 de Marzo tugboat
description:
A tugboat named 13 de Marzo carrying 72 Cuban refugees was deliberately sunk by Cuban government vessels in Havana Harbor, killing 37 people including children. The incident was condemned internationally but no one was prosecuted.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/13_de_Marzo_(tugboat))

title: Execution of Arnaldo Ochoa
description:
General Arnaldo Ochoa, a decorated Cuban military hero, was arrested, tried, and executed for drug trafficking and treason in Havana. Many observers considered the trial politically motivated. The case exposed drug smuggling operations.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arnaldo_Ochoa)

title: Brothers to the Rescue shootdown
description:
Two civilian aircraft operated by Brothers to the Rescue were shot down by Cuban Air Force MiG-29s over international waters near Havana, killing four crew members. Cuban intelligence agent Juan Pablo Roque had infiltrated the group.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1996_shootdown_of_Brothers_to_the_Rescue_aircraft)

title: Murder of Orlando Jorge Mera
description:
Dominican Republic Environment Minister Orlando Jorge Mera was shot dead in his office in Santo Domingo by a childhood friend, Miguel Cruz, during a meeting. Cruz was arrested at the scene and charged with murder.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Orlando_Jorge_Mera)

title: Shooting of David Ortiz
description:
Former Boston Red Sox star David Ortiz was shot in the back at a Santo Domingo nightclub. Multiple suspects were arrested, and the shooting was determined to be a case of mistaken identity linked to a Dominican drug lord.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shooting_of_David_Ortiz)

title: Murder of the Mirabal sisters
description:
Three Mirabal sisters, political activists opposing the Trujillo dictatorship, were assassinated on orders of Dominican dictator Rafael Trujillo. Their deaths became a symbol of resistance. November 25 is now the International Day for the Elimination of Violence Against Women.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mirabal_sisters)

title: Murder of Keron Hewitt and others - Tivoli Gardens incursion
description:
A military operation to arrest drug lord Christopher 'Dudus' Coke in the Tivoli Gardens area of Kingston resulted in the deaths of at least 73 civilians. The incursion raised serious human rights concerns and prompted a public inquiry.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2010_Kingston_unrest)

title: Extradition of Christopher 'Dudus' Coke
description:
Jamaican drug lord Christopher 'Dudus' Coke was captured after an extensive manhunt and extradited to the US. He pleaded guilty to racketeering and drug trafficking charges and was sentenced to 23 years in prison.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Christopher_Coke)

title: Murder of Peter Tosh
description:
Reggae legend Peter Tosh was shot and killed during a home invasion robbery at his Kingston residence. Three men, including Dennis 'Leppo' Lobban, invaded the home. Lobban was convicted and sentenced to death.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peter_Tosh)

title: Murder of Selwyn Richardson
description:
The kidnapping and murder of businessman Selwyn Richardson in Trinidad highlighted the country's kidnapping crisis. Multiple suspects were arrested and convicted. The case prompted the government to strengthen anti-kidnapping legislation.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crime_in_Trinidad_and_Tobago)

title: 1990 Trinidadian coup attempt
description:
Members of the Jamaat al Muslimeen, led by Yasin Abu Bakr, stormed the Trinidad and Tobago Parliament and the TTT television station, holding the Prime Minister and others hostage for six days. 24 people were killed.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jamaat_al_Muslimeen_coup_attempt)

title: Murder of Dana Seetahal
description:
Prominent Trinidad and Tobago Senior Counsel Dana Seetahal was shot and killed in her vehicle in Port of Spain. She had been involved in prosecuting high-profile criminal cases. Despite arrests, the case remains unresolved.

[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dana_Seetahal)
